detail.title: Cross-Cutting Rules, Interface Contracts, and Non-Functional Requirements

requirement.title: R1 — Human Consent for Raw Source
requirement.description: Design docs (raw source) MUST NOT be created or modified without human consent or initiation. The LLM assists in elicitation but the human controls when a design doc is committed.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: A design doc MUST NOT be written to disk until the user explicitly confirms the content (e.g., "looks good", "commit it", section-by-section approval). REQ-F024
After a design doc is committed, it MUST NOT be modified. Any change requires a new iteration (new date folder, new design.md). REQ-F024

requirement.title: R2 — Unidirectional Flow
requirement.description: Downstream stages MUST NOT modify upstream artifacts. Refiner cannot modify design docs; planner cannot modify spec.xml.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Refiner MUST NOT write to any file under docs/plans/. Its only output paths are specs/<spec-id>/ (on success) and docs/plans/<spec-id>/<date>/refiner-report.md (on block; this is a report alongside the design, not a modification of it). REQ-F025
Planner MUST NOT write to specs/<spec-id>/spec.xml or specs/<spec-id>/details/. Its only output paths are specs/<spec-id>/dag.yaml and specs/<spec-id>/epics/. REQ-F025

requirement.title: Design Doc Contract
requirement.description: The design doc is the complete output of brainstorming and the complete input to the refiner. The refiner consumes the entire design doc — no special section or structured summary is needed. This contract defines the design doc structure for Path A (new spec) and Path B (iteration).

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Design docs MUST be located at docs/plans/<spec-id>/<YYYY-MM-DD>/design.md. The spec-id is derived from the directory name. The iteration date is derived from the date directory name. REQ-F003

Path A (new spec) design docs MUST contain substantive design content: at minimum, a description of the problem, proposed solution, and identifiable requirements (in prose). Empty or stub design docs are ERROR. REQ-F003

Path B (iteration) design docs MUST follow gamma mode structure with at minimum: a Context section (summarizing current spec state with version reference) and a Change Intent section (describing what is changing and why). Missing Context or Change Intent is ERROR. REQ-F005
The refiner determines processing mode from filesystem state, not from fields in the design doc: specs/<spec-id>/spec.xml exists = iteration mode; does not exist = initial mode. REQ-F003, REQ-F005

requirement.title: Spec Identity Header Contract
requirement.description: The spec.xml identity header is the interface contract between refiner (producer) and planner (consumer). This is the single source of truth for the header format.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Identity header MUST contain: spec_id (string), spec_version (positive integer), status ("active"), source/design_path (valid file path), source/design_iteration (YYYY-MM-DD). Missing fields are ERROR. REQ-F010
source/design_path MUST point to an existing design doc file. A path pointing to a non-existent file is ERROR. REQ-F010
scope MUST list included features with IDs and excluded items with reasons. Empty scope is WARNING. REQ-F010
When spec_version > 1, supersedes MUST be set to "<spec-id>:v<previous-version>". Missing supersedes for v2+ is ERROR. REQ-F010

requirement.title: SDD Schemas (Guidance Layer)
requirement.description: Each pipeline artifact MUST have a corresponding schema file in scripts/lib/sdd-schemas/ that serves as the format guide for LLMs. Schema files are the guidance layer — they tell the LLM what format to produce. They live in scripts/lib/ (shared infrastructure) so any skill can read them without cross-skill coupling. Full schema content definitions are in details/sdd-schemas.xml (fr-sd-001 through fr-sd-007).

acceptance_criteria.criterion: scripts/lib/sdd-schemas/design.md MUST exist and conform to the design doc schema definition (fr-sd-001 through fr-sd-004). REQ-F003, REQ-F005
scripts/lib/sdd-schemas/spec.md MUST exist and conform to the spec identity header schema definition (fr-sd-005 through fr-sd-007). REQ-F010
Schema files are the guidance layer only. They MUST NOT be the enforcement mechanism — enforcement is delegated to sdd-utils.js (fr-cc-if-004). REQ-F008

requirement.title: SDD Utils (Validation Toolkit)
requirement.description: Deterministic validation and information checks MUST be implemented in scripts/lib/sdd-utils.js. This module is a validation toolkit — it validates structure and provides information, but does NOT merge or author spec content (the LLM manages spec content directly, like maintaining a wiki page). Full API definitions are in details/sdd-schemas.xml (fr-sd-008, fr-sd-009). LLM judgment is reserved for semantic checks (contradictions, ambiguity).

acceptance_criteria.criterion: sdd-utils.js MUST export the design doc validator API defined in fr-sd-008 (parseDesignDoc, validateDesignDoc). REQ-F003, REQ-F005
sdd-utils.js MUST export the spec header validator API defined in fr-sd-009 (parseSpecHeader, validateSpecHeader). REQ-F010, REQ-F016
Semantic checks NOT implemented by this module (delegated to LLM): contradiction detection between requirements, ambiguity assessment, scope completeness judgment. REQ-F008, REQ-F015
When a schema file (fr-cc-if-003) and sdd-utils.js disagree on what is valid, sdd-utils.js wins. Schema files guide production; sdd-utils.js enforces correctness. REQ-F003, REQ-F010

requirement.title: Skill Access Pattern
requirement.description: Each pipeline skill accesses the contract through two layers: scripts/lib/sdd-schemas/ for format guidance (what to produce) and scripts/lib/sdd-utils.js for programmatic validation (is it correct). Both layers are shared infrastructure. No skill reads another skill's files.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: arc-brainstorming SKILL.md MUST read scripts/lib/sdd-schemas/design.md before producing the design doc, to ensure the output conforms to the design doc contract (Path A or Path B gamma mode structure). REQ-F003

arc-refining SKILL.md MUST invoke scripts/lib/sdd-utils.js validateDesignDoc() for deterministic input validation before any formalization. If valid is false with ERROR issues, refiner MUST block and produce a refiner-report.md. REQ-F008

arc-refining SKILL.md MUST read scripts/lib/sdd-schemas/spec.md when producing spec.xml, and invoke scripts/lib/sdd-utils.js validateSpecHeader() for deterministic output validation. If valid is false, refiner MUST NOT write any files. REQ-F010, REQ-F015

arc-planning SKILL.md MUST invoke scripts/lib/sdd-utils.js validateSpecHeader() for spec.xml input validation before any decomposition. Planner MUST NOT read the design doc — it works from the spec only. Rationale: the three-layer model requires that the derived layer (DAG) reads only from the wiki layer (spec), never from the raw source layer (design doc). The spec's <delta> metadata provides the planning scope that would otherwise require reading the design doc. REQ-F016

requirement.title: Validation Toolkit API Self-Documentation
requirement.description: The exported function signatures of scripts/lib/sdd-utils.js (parseDesignDoc, validateDesignDoc, parseSpecHeader, validateSpecHeader) reveal the expected input/output schemas through their destructuring patterns and return types. This exposure is intentional — it makes the validation toolkit self-documenting for both human maintainers and LLM agents exploring the codebase. Any consumer reading the module can infer schema requirements (spec_id, spec_version, supersedes, delta structure, etc.) without reading sdd-schemas/ files. This property has testing-methodology implications documented in nfr-003.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: A consumer (human or LLM agent) reading scripts/lib/sdd-utils.js MUST be able to identify the expected fields of a spec identity header (spec_id, spec_version, supersedes, delta structure, added/modified/removed/renamed children) from the parseSpecHeader return destructuring alone, without consulting any external documentation. REQ-F010

When sdd-utils.js function signatures change (adding, removing, or renaming returned fields), the format contracts in scripts/lib/sdd-schemas/ MUST be updated in the same iteration. The two layers describe the same underlying schema — sdd-utils enforces it, sdd-schemas documents it. Drift between them is ERROR. REQ-F010

requirement.title: Two-Pass Write Pattern
requirement.description: Stages that produce file artifacts (refiner, planner) MUST follow the two-pass write pattern: (1) build output in memory, (2) validate output against the relevant interface contract, (3) write to disk only if validation passes. This prevents partially written or invalid artifacts from reaching the filesystem.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Refiner MUST NOT write spec.xml or details/ files until the complete output has been built in memory and validated against fr-cc-if-002. REQ-F012, REQ-F015
Planner MUST NOT write dag.yaml or epics/ until the complete output has been built in memory and validated for structural integrity. REQ-F017
When output validation fails, the stage MUST report all ERROR findings with remediation guidance and produce no output files. REQ-F008, REQ-F015

requirement.title: Validation Report with Remediation
requirement.description: When a stage blocks due to validation failure, the report MUST include not just what failed but how to fix it. This follows OpenSpec's enriched error message pattern.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Each ERROR finding MUST include: the specific field or section that failed, the expected format (referencing the interface contract), and a concrete remediation suggestion. REQ-F009
WARNING findings MUST be reported to the user but MUST NOT block the stage from producing output. REQ-F015

requirement.title: Path-Agnostic Skill Descriptions
requirement.description: Pipeline skill descriptions (frontmatter) MUST NOT hardcode artifact paths. Triggering conditions MUST describe intent and context, not file locations. The user provides the spec-id or target via their prompt.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: arc-brainstorming, arc-refining, and arc-planning descriptions MUST NOT contain literal paths like "specs/spec.xml" or "specs/<spec-id>/spec.xml". They MUST describe triggering conditions in terms of user intent (e.g., "when converting design documents to structured specs"). REQ-N001
Each pipeline skill MUST accept a spec-id from the user's prompt as the primary routing input. The skill resolves the spec-id to the correct artifact paths internally. REQ-N001
When the user does not provide a spec-id, the skill MUST scan specs/ and docs/plans/ to present available targets and ask the user to choose. REQ-N001

requirement.title: Existing Infrastructure Compatibility
requirement.description: The SDD pipeline MUST work with arcforge's existing CLI and skill infrastructure. No new runtime dependencies.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: All pipeline operations MUST be invocable via existing arcforge skill mechanism (arc-brainstorming, arc-refining, arc-planning) or their updated versions. REQ-N001
No new npm packages MUST be added to package.json dependencies. devDependencies are exempt. REQ-N001

requirement.title: File-Based Artifacts
requirement.description: All artifacts MUST be file-based (YAML, XML, Markdown). No database, no server, no external state store.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Pipeline state MUST be fully derivable from files on disk. Running `ls` and `cat` on the specs/ and docs/plans/ directories MUST be sufficient to determine the complete pipeline state. REQ-N002
No pipeline operation MUST require network access, a running server, or a database connection. REQ-N002

requirement.title: Skill Adherence Evaluation Methodology
requirement.description: SDD pipeline skills (arc-brainstorming, arc-refining, arc-planning) cannot be reliably evaluated via A/B comparison (baseline vs treatment) on format-correctness output. The self-documenting nature of sdd-utils.js (fr-cc-if-006) means a competent baseline agent can infer the correct output format by reading the module, closing the delta between baseline and treatment. This property was empirically confirmed during the 2026-04-17 eval session against Claude Sonnet 4.x and Opus 4.x models.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: A/B evaluation of SDD pipeline skills measuring "does treatment produce correct spec.xml format?" WILL yield delta near zero with current-generation LLM agents. This is a consequence of model capability combined with fr-cc-if-006, not a skill deficiency. Eval designers MUST NOT interpret zero delta here as "skill adds no value." REQ-N001

The preferred eval form for SDD pipeline skills is single-condition agent evaluation (pass@k on treatment trials only), measuring reliability across k trials rather than behavioral delta against a non-discriminative baseline. SHIP threshold: pass rate >= 80% across k >= 5 trials after accounting for harness noise (grader failures, max_turns truncation). REQ-N001

Behaviors that CAN be discriminatively A/B tested for these skills are those NOT inferable from sdd-utils.js source alone — examples include: DAG completion gate blocking behavior (fr-pl-007), refiner-report.md production on contradiction (fr-rf-001), error recovery ordering, block-vs-proceed decisions. These remain candidates for focused A/B eval when discrimination is required. REQ-N001
